steps.title: Cell culture medium Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: Cell culture medium Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume Quantity or Volume FBS 10% 50 mL 56.8 mL L-glutamine 1% 5 mL 5.7 mL Penicillin-streptomycin 1% 5 mL 5.7 mL DMEM n/a 440 mL 500 mL Keep at 4 °C.

0.1 M phosphate buffer, pH 7.4 Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume Sodium dihydrogen phosphate 0.1 M 96.5 mL of 0.2 M stock solution Disodium hydrogen phosphate 0.1 M 153.5 mL of 0.2 M stock solution dH 2 O n/a 200 mL 5 M HCl or 5 M NaOH pH 7.4 As needed to adjust pH to 7.4 dH 2 O n/a Up to 500 mL dH 2 O: deionized H 2 O Stock solutions a. 0.2 M Sodium dihydrogen phosphate Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume NaH 2 PO 4 ·H 2 O 0.2 M 27.598 g (MW: 137.99 g/mol) dH 2 O n/a Up to 1 L Store at room temperature. b. 0.2 M Disodium hydrogen phosphate Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume Na 2 HPO 4 ·7H 2 O 0.2 M 53.614 g (MW: 268.07 g/mol) dH 2 O n/a Up to 1 L Store at room temperature.

steps.title: 5 M NaCl Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: 5 M NaCl Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume NaCl 5 M 146.1 g NaCl (MW: 58.44 g/mol) dH 2 O n/a Up to 500 mL Store at room temperature.

steps.title: RIPA phosphate lysis buffer with protease...
steps.instruction: RIPA phosphate lysis buffer with protease and phosphatase inhibitors To the RIPA phosphate lysis buffer base, add protease and phosphatase inhibitors as indicated. Protease/phosphatase inhibitors are prepared as stock solutions in distilled water, stored frozen, and added into the lysis buffer just prior to use as follows: Reagent Stock solution concentration Volume per 1 mL lysis buffer Aprotinin 1 mg/mL 5 μL Leupeptin 10 mg/mL 5 μL Sodium fluoride (NaF) 0.2 M 5 μL Sodium vanadate (Na 3 VO 4 ) 0.2 M 5 μL PMSF 0.1 M 15 μL β-glycerophosphate 2 M 40 μL Alternatively, dissolve one tablet of Complete Mini EDTA-free protease inhibitor cocktail in 10 mL of phosphate RIPA lysis buffer base. Aliquot and keep at -20 °C. Phosphate RIPA lysis buffer base (50 mL) Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume Phosphate buffer, pH 7.4 10 mM 5 mL of 0.1 M stock solution NaCl 150 mM 1.5 mL of 5 M NaCl NP-40 1% 0.714 g TERGITOL TM solution (70% NP-40 in H 2 O) DTT 5 mM 38.575 mg DTT Sodium deoxycholate 0.1% 50 mg dH 2 O n/a Up to 50 mL The lysis buffer base is kept at 4 °C.

steps.title: 200 mM DTT Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: 200 mM DTT Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume DTT 200 mM 15.43 mg (MW: 154.3 g/mol) dH 2 0 n/a Up to 500 μL Prepare fresh just before use.

steps.title: 500 mM pPDA with 5 mM...
steps.instruction: 500 mM pPDA with 5 mM DTT Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume pPDA 500 mM 55.17 mg pPDA (MW: 108.14 g/mol) DMSO n/a 97.5 μL DTT 200 mM 2.5 μL For easier preparation of the reagent needed for multiple labeling reactions, it is recommended to weigh a larger amount and then adjust the volume of DMSO accordingly. For instance, if 200 mg of pPDA is placed in a 1.5 mL Eppendorf tube, 353.45 μL of DMSO should be added and vortexed until fully dissolved. Subsequently, for each 97.5 μL of this solution, 2.5 μL of freshly prepared 200 mM DTT should be added to create the final solution.

steps.title: 100 mM biotin-aminooxy Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: 100 mM biotin-aminooxy Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume EZ-Link Alkoxyamine-PEG4-Biotin 100 mM 50.0 mg (MW: 154.3 g/mol) DMSO n/a 1,151 μL Dissolve biotin-aminooxy in DMSO, make small, single-experiment aliquots, and store at -80 °C. Avoid multiple freeze-thaw cycles. This solution is stable for at least one year.

steps.title: 4× SDS-PAGE loading buffer (reducing) Reagent...
steps.instruction: 4× SDS-PAGE loading buffer (reducing) Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume 0.5 M Tris-HCl, pH 6.8 0.25 M 5 mL Glycerol 40% (v/v) 4 mL SDS 8% (w/v) 0.8 g 2-Mercaptoethanol (2-ME) 4% (v/v) 0.4 mL Bromophenol blue 0.01% 4 mg dH 2 O n/a Up to 10 mL

steps.title: 10% Ammonium persulfate solution Reagent Final...
steps.instruction: 10% Ammonium persulfate solution Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume Ammonium persulfate (APS) 10% 100.0 mg dH 2 O n/a 1 mL Prepare fresh for each experiment.

steps.title: 10% SDS Reagent Final concentration Quantity...
steps.instruction: 10% SDS Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume Sodium dodecyl sulfate (SDS) 10% 10.0 g dH 2 O n/a Up to 100 mL Store at room temperature.

steps.title: Resolving gel buffer, 1
steps.instruction: Resolving gel buffer, 1.5 M Tris-HCl, pH 8.8 Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume Tris base 1.5 M 181.7 g dH 2 O n/a Up to 800 mL HCl (37%) pH 8.8 As needed to adjust pH to pH 8.8 dH 2 O n/a Up to 1 L Store at 4 °C.

steps.title: Stacking gel buffer, 0
steps.instruction: Stacking gel buffer, 0.5 M Tris-HCl, pH 6.8 Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume Tris base 0.5 M 60.6 g dH 2 O n/a Up to 800 mL HCl (37%) pH 6.8 As needed to adjust pH to pH 6.8 dH 2 O n/a Up to 1 L Store at 4 °C.

steps.title: 10× SDS-PAGE running buffer Reagent Final...
steps.instruction: 10× SDS-PAGE running buffer Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume Tris base 0.25 M 30.29 g (MW: 121.14 g/mol) Glycine 1.92 M 144.13 g (MW: 75.07 g/mol) SDS 0.1% 10 mL from 10% SDS dH 2 O n/a 1 L Dissolve in 900 mL of dH 2 O, adjust to 1 L with dH 2 O, and store at room temperature. The buffer has a pH of around 8.

steps.title: Do not adjust its pH
steps.instruction: Do not adjust its pH.

steps.title: 1× SDS-PAGE running buffer Reagent Final...
steps.instruction: 1× SDS-PAGE running buffer Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume 10× SDS-PAGE running buffer 1× 100 mL SDS 0.1% 10 mL of 10% SDS solution dH 2 O n/a Up to 1 L Keep at room temperature. If used infrequently, store at 4 °C.

steps.title: Western blotting transfer buffer Reagent Final...
steps.instruction: Western blotting transfer buffer Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume Tris base 25 mM 3.03 g (MW: 121,14 g/mol) Glycine 0.192 M 14.4 g (MW: 75.07 g/mol) Methanol 20% 200 mL dH 2 O n/a Up to 1 L Prepare on the day of transfer and cool down at -20 °C while running the SDS-PAGE.

steps.title: Ponceau S staining solution Reagent Final...
steps.instruction: Ponceau S staining solution Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume Ponceau S 0.1% (wt/vol) 0.05 g Glacial acetic acid 5% (v/v) 2.5 mL dH 2 O n/a Up to 50 mL Add ultrapure water up to 50 mL, wrap in aluminum foil, and store at room temperature.

steps.title: 10× Tris-buffered saline, Tween-20 (TBS-T) Reagent...
steps.instruction: 10× Tris-buffered saline, Tween-20 (TBS-T) Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume Tris base 50 mM 6.05 g (MW: 121.14 g/mol) NaCl 150 mM 8.76 g (MW: 58.44 g/mol) HCl (37%) pH 7.5 Adjust to pH 7.5 Tween-20 0.1 % 1 mL dH 2 O n/a Up to 1 L Add ultrapure water up to 800 mL and set the pH value up to 7.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of Tween-20 and...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of Tween-20 and add water up to 1,000 mL. Store at 4 °C.

steps.title: Blocking solution Reagent Final concentration Quantity...
steps.instruction: Blocking solution Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume Nonfat dry milk 5% 2.5 g 10× TBS-T 1× 5 mL dH 2 O n/a Up to 50 mL Prepare on the day of transfer and keep at 4 °C.

steps.title: 1:1,000 Streptavidin-HRP conjugate solution Reagent Final...
steps.instruction: 1:1,000 Streptavidin-HRP conjugate solution Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume Streptavidin-HRP 1‰ 10 μL 10× TBS-T 1× 1 mL dH 2 O n/a Up to 10 mL Mix well. Prepare just prior to the incubation with the membrane.

steps.title: ECL detection solution Reagent Final concentration...
steps.instruction: ECL detection solution Reagent Final concentration Quantity or Volume Pierce ECL Solution A 50% 2.0 mL Pierce ECL Solution B 50% 2.0 mL Mix the solutions just before use. For a typical size of the membrane in Mini-Protean Tetra Cell (9 × 7 cm), 4 mL of ECL detection solution is sufficient.

steps.title: Resolving gel solution Gel density Milli-Q...
steps.instruction: Resolving gel solution Gel density Milli-Q water (mL) 30% acrylamide/ bis acrylamide (mL) Resolving gel buffer (mL) 10% SDS (μL) 7% 5.1 2.3 2.5 100 8% 4.7 2.7 2.5 100 9% 4.4 3.0 2.5 100 10% 4.1 3.3 2.5 100 11% 3.7 3.7 2.5 100 12% 3.4 4.0 2.5 100 Add 50 µL of 10% APC and 5 µL of TEMED to each 10 mL of the resolving gel solution. Gently mix, then pour the mixture into the gel assembly.

steps.title: 4% stacking gel solution Gel density...
steps.instruction: 4% stacking gel solution Gel density Milli-Q water (mL) 30% acrylamide/ bis acrylamide (mL) Stacking gel buffer (mL) 10% SDS (μL) 4% 6.1 1.3 2.5 100 Add 50 µL of 10% APC and 5 µL of TEMED to each 10 mL of the stacking gel solution. Gently mix, then pour the mixture into the gel assembly. Laboratory supplies

steps.instruction: T75 cell culture flasks (Sarstedt AG & Co. KG, catalog number: 83.3911.

steps.instruction: Mini Trans-Blot ® filter paper (Bio-Rad, catalog number:

steps.title: Culture A549 cells in cell culture...
steps.instruction: Culture A549 cells in cell culture medium (see Recipes) in a humidified atmosphere with 5% CO 2 at 37 °C until they reach 80% confluence. B. Cell lysis and protein quantification

steps.title: Remove the cell culture medium and...
steps.instruction: Remove the cell culture medium and rinse the flask with 10 mL of ice-cold PBS. Discard the PBS, add 1.5 mL of Trypsin EDTA, and incubate at 37 °C for about 5 min.

steps.title: Swirl the flask and gently tap...
steps.instruction: Swirl the flask and gently tap to verify that cells have begun to detach.

steps.title: Add 9 mL of cell culture...
steps.instruction: Add 9 mL of cell culture medium to neutralize the trypsin and collect detached cells.

steps.title: Centrifuge the cell suspension at 300×...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the cell suspension at 300× g for 10 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Discard the supernatant and wash the...
steps.instruction: Discard the supernatant and wash the cells 2× with PBS as described above.

steps.title: Add 0
steps.instruction: Add 0.1 mL of lysis buffer (see Recipes) per 10 7 cells.

steps.title: Incubate on ice for 30 min...
steps.instruction: Incubate on ice for 30 min while vortexing for 30 s every 10 min.

steps.title: Centrifuge the lysate at 15,000× g...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the lysate at 15,000× g for 15 min at 4 °C.

steps.title: Transfer the supernatant into a new 1
steps.instruction: Transfer the supernatant into a new 1.5 mL microcentrifuge tube.

steps.title: Determine the protein concentration
steps.instruction: Determine the protein concentration. In this work, we did it through OD measurement at 280 nm on a BioDrop TM μLite+, but it could also be done using a BCA assay from Thermo Fisher Scientific.

steps.title: Adjust the protein concentration to about...
steps.instruction: Adjust the protein concentration to about 5 mg/mL using the RIPA phosphate lysis buffer with protease and phosphatase inhibitors. C. Carbonyl labeling

steps.title: Set up the labeling reaction by...
steps.instruction: Set up the labeling reaction by combining the following in the 1.5 mL (or 2.0 mL) Eppendorf tube: 80 μL of cell lysate 10 μL of 100 mM aminooxy-biotin (see Recipes) 10 μL of 500 mM pPDA with 5 mM DTT (see Recipes)

steps.title: Set aside a negative control tube...
steps.instruction: Set aside a negative control tube containing 80 μL of cell lysate and 20 μL of RIPA phosphate lysis buffer with protease and phosphatase inhibitors (no aminooxy-biotin or pPDA/DTT added).

steps.title: Incubate the samples in a thermomixer...
steps.instruction: Incubate the samples in a thermomixer at 25 °C and 500 rpm for 3 h.

steps.title: After incubation, add 33
steps.instruction: After incubation, add 33.3 μL of 4× loading buffer (see Recipes) to each sample.

steps.title: Denature the samples by heating them...
steps.instruction: Denature the samples by heating them at 95 °C for 5 min.

steps.title: Use immediately for the Oxime blot...
steps.instruction: Use immediately for the Oxime blot analysis or store at -20 °C until further analysis. D. Oxime blot analysis D

steps.title: Assemble the glass plates for gel...
steps.instruction: Assemble the glass plates for gel casting and ensure that the bottoms are even.

steps.title: Insert into the casting stand and...
steps.instruction: Insert into the casting stand and check for leaks by adding a small amount of dH 2 O.

steps.title: Pour out the liquid and dry the space
steps.instruction: Pour out the liquid and dry the space.

steps.title: Prepare a resolving gel (see Recipes)
steps.instruction: Prepare a resolving gel (see Recipes). Stir briefly and gently pipette approximately 5.5 mL (1.5 mm gel) of the solution. Make sure to have about 5 mm from the bottom of the comb to the resolving gel.

steps.title: Add 1 mL of isopropanol on...
steps.instruction: Add 1 mL of isopropanol on top of the gel and wait until it polymerizes.

steps.title: Decant the isopropanol and rinse with...
steps.instruction: Decant the isopropanol and rinse with the dH 2 O to remove any residual isopropanol. Drain thoroughly and wipe the water from the plates before pouring the stacking gel.

steps.title: Pour the 4% stacking gel (see Recipes)
steps.instruction: Pour the 4% stacking gel (see Recipes). Insert the combs at an angle to avoid trapping any bubbles below the bottom of the well.

steps.title: Wait until the stacking gel polymerizes (about 30 min)
steps.instruction: Wait until the stacking gel polymerizes (about 30 min). It is convenient to prepare the gels in advance. When kept in moist paper tissue in a tightly closed container at 4 °C, they can be used for several days.

steps.title: Prepare 1× SDS-PAGE running buffer (see Recipes)
steps.instruction: Prepare 1× SDS-PAGE running buffer (see Recipes).

steps.title: Remove the comb and wash the...
steps.instruction: Remove the comb and wash the wells with 1× SDS-PAGE running buffer 2–3 times, draining in between the washes.

steps.title: Assemble the electrode assembly cassette, fill...
steps.instruction: Assemble the electrode assembly cassette, fill it with the running buffer, and ensure it does not leak.

steps.title: Load the prestained markers (3–4 μL)...
steps.instruction: Load the prestained markers (3–4 μL) and samples (10–30 μL, depending on the protein content) into the wells.

steps.title: Add running buffer into the lower...
steps.instruction: Add running buffer into the lower buffer chamber until the lower end of the gel plates is about 1 cm immersed in the buffer.

steps.title: Initiate the electrophoresis at 100 V...
steps.instruction: Initiate the electrophoresis at 100 V until the samples cross from the stacking to the resolving gel.

steps.title: Increase the voltage to 150 V...
steps.instruction: Increase the voltage to 150 V and continue protein separation until the front dye reaches the bottom of the gel.

steps.title: Disassemble the electrode assembly and separate the glass plates
steps.instruction: Disassemble the electrode assembly and separate the glass plates.

steps.title: Remove the stacking from the resolving...
steps.instruction: Remove the stacking from the resolving gel using a gel spatula or glass plate.

steps.title: Use the gels to visualize protein...
steps.instruction: Use the gels to visualize protein separation by Coomassie blue staining or for protein transfers and subsequent detection of carbonylated proteins by Oxime blot. D

steps.title: Coomassie blue staining
steps.instruction: Coomassie blue staining

steps.title: Mix the InstantBlue ® Coomassie protein...
steps.instruction: Mix the InstantBlue ® Coomassie protein stain by gently inverting the bottle a few times immediately before use.

steps.title: Completely immerse the gel in about...
steps.instruction: Completely immerse the gel in about 20 mL of InstantBlue ® stain and incubate at room temperature with gentle shaking until the required intensity is achieved (usually 30–60 min).

steps.title: Briefly rinse with dH 2 O
steps.instruction: Briefly rinse with dH 2 O.

steps.title: Image the stained gel with an...
steps.instruction: Image the stained gel with an imaging system such as ChemiDoc TM MP imaging system and analyze using software such as Image Lab ( Figures 1A , 2A , and Figure S1A Extended data). Figure

steps.title: Spontaneous carbonylation of BSA
steps.instruction: Spontaneous carbonylation of BSA. BSA dissolved at 5 mg/mL in dH 2 O was incubated with either aminooxy-biotin and pPDA catalyst (+) or an equal volume of 10 mM phosphate buffer (-). Equal quantities of unlabeled (-) and labeled (+) BSA (10, 5, and 2.5 μg/lane, respectively) were resolved in adjacent lanes by SDS-PAGE and analyzed by (A) Coomassie blue staining for the total protein or (B) Ponceau S staining to confirm the protein transfer to the PVDF membrane and (C) Oxime blot to analyze the BSA carbonylation. Figure

steps.title: Oxime blot of A549 cell lysate proteins
steps.instruction: Oxime blot of A549 cell lysate proteins. Proteins extracted from A549 cells were derivatized (+) with aminooxy-biotin in the presence of the pPDA catalyst or used as a negative control (-) for samples treated with an equal volume of the phosphate buffer. A decreasing amount of total proteins (from 46 to 5.75 μg/lane) were resolved by SDS-PAGE proteins and analyzed by (A) Coomassie blue staining for the total protein pattern or following the transfer to the PVDF membrane by (B) Ponceau S staining to confirm the protein transfer and (C) Oxime blot to analyze the carbonylation of endogenous A549 proteins. D

steps.title: Oxime blot analysis
steps.instruction: Oxime blot analysis

steps.title: Activate the PVDF membrane by soaking...
steps.instruction: Activate the PVDF membrane by soaking it in 100% methanol for 1 min.

steps.title: Rinse with dH 2 O for 2–3 min
steps.instruction: Rinse with dH 2 O for 2–3 min.

steps.title: Incubate in transfer buffer until use
steps.instruction: Incubate in transfer buffer until use.

steps.title: Immerse sponge pads in the previously...
steps.instruction: Immerse sponge pads in the previously cooled transfer buffer to facilitate the removal of the bubbles.

steps.title: Assemble the transfer sandwich
steps.instruction: Assemble the transfer sandwich. The cathode side of the transfer sandwich on the Bio-Rad device is marked in black. The correct order from the black transfer sandwich sheet is as follows: sponge pad, filter paper, gel, PVDF membrane, filter paper, sponge pad from cathode to anode. Remove bubbles between the gel and membrane using a round object such as a part of a 10 or 50-mL plastic pipette.

steps.title: Place the sandwich in the electrode...
steps.instruction: Place the sandwich in the electrode module, observing the correct orientation relative to the electrical current direction [black, black transfer sandwich sheet closer to the black (cathode) side of the electrode module].

steps.title: Pour the cold transfer buffer into...
steps.instruction: Pour the cold transfer buffer into the transfer tank with the top level just below the top of the electrode module.

steps.title: Place the transfer tank in an...
steps.instruction: Place the transfer tank in an insulated Styrofoam box and surround it with ice and ice-cold water. Sporadically add ice to the mix to ensure efficient cooling.

steps.title: Transfer proteins from the gel to...
steps.instruction: Transfer proteins from the gel to the PVDF membrane at 400 mA for 2 h (for two gels).

steps.title: Check that proteins are transferred by...
steps.instruction: Check that proteins are transferred by staining the blotted membrane with Ponceau S solution (see Recipes).

steps.title: Immerse the membrane in the Ponceau...
steps.instruction: Immerse the membrane in the Ponceau S solution with gentle shaking for 5–15 min (until proteins are easily visible).

steps.title: Decant the stain in the original...
steps.instruction: Decant the stain in the original container, since the dye can be reused for several weeks.

steps.title: Remove the bulk of the stain...
steps.instruction: Remove the bulk of the stain with 2–3 quick rinses with dH 2 O while shaking.

steps.title: De-stain the membrane by incubating it...
steps.instruction: De-stain the membrane by incubating it for 5 min in dH 2 O on a 3D orbital shaker at 30 rpm.

steps.title: Take images on the imaging system...
steps.instruction: Take images on the imaging system if required for record keeping ( Figures 1B and 2B ).

steps.title: Block the nonspecific binding of the...
steps.instruction: Block the nonspecific binding of the aminooxy-biotin by incubating the membrane in blocking solution (see Recipes) for 1 h at room temperature with gentle shaking on a 3D orbital shaker at 30 rpm. Pause point: Blocking could be extended overnight if convenient. Wash the membrane twice with 1× TBS-T (see Recipes) for 5 min each.

steps.title: Wash the membrane twice with 1×...
steps.instruction: Wash the membrane twice with 1× TBS-T (see Recipes) for 5 min each.

steps.title: Prepare 10 mL of 1:1,000 Streptavidin...
steps.instruction: Prepare 10 mL of 1:1,000 Streptavidin (HRP conjugate) solution (see Recipes) for each membrane.

steps.title: Incubate for 1 h at room...
steps.instruction: Incubate for 1 h at room temperature with gentle shaking on a 3D orbital shaker at 30 rpm.

steps.title: Wash the membrane 3× with 15–20...
steps.instruction: Wash the membrane 3× with 15–20 mL of 1× TBS-T (see Recipes) for 5–10 min each.

steps.title: Prepare 4 mL of ECL detection...
steps.instruction: Prepare 4 mL of ECL detection solution (see Recipes) for each membrane and use immediately.

steps.title: Apply the detection solution to the...
steps.instruction: Apply the detection solution to the membrane and take the image on the imaging system ( Figures 1C , 2C , and Figure S1B Extended data).

description: Validation of protocol This protocol was developed and validated in the research article by Ladouce et al. [16] (see Figures 2A and 3C).
